topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and jarring, right in the middle of my study session. It was a post from Mr. Harrison, my notoriously strict history teacher. A picture of him, smiling broadly, captioned with a cryptic quote about the importance of 'connecting with the current generation.' My jaw tightened. I clicked on the profile, my thumb hovering over the 'unfollow' button, but my eyes snagged on the recent story—a blurry snapshot taken at a local coffee shop, and I recognized the angle of the building behind him. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. He wasn't just observing; he was actively documenting. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet library corner, and felt a sharp, burning resentment bubble up in my chest.

[story 2]

I was scrolling mindlessly through Instagram, trying to escape the suffocating pressure of impending exams, when I saw it. Mr. Davies, my English professor, had tagged me in a story featuring a meme about procrastination. The caption read, "Still wrestling with those deadlines, [MyUsername]." A sudden wave of icy frustration washed over me. I felt my shoulders tense up, my knuckles whitening as I gripped my phone. Why? What was he trying to achieve by broadcasting my struggles? I quickly navigated to his profile, a cold knot forming in my stomach. He seemed so casual, so entitled to this level of access. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to keep my expression neutral, but the heat rising in my cheeks betrayed the churning feeling of being scrutinized.

[story 3]

It happened during a casual browsing session. I stumbled across a photo on my feed—a candid shot of Ms. Evans, my calculus instructor, laughing with a group of colleagues. Then, a new notification appeared: Ms. Evans had liked my most recent post, a thoughtful analysis of a complex theorem. Immediately following that, a direct message popped up. It was from her. "Really enjoyed your perspective on the derivative," she typed. The words felt like a deliberate dismissal. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in my throat. I felt a distinct clenching in my gut, a tightening sensation that made my hands tremble slightly. I quickly minimized the chat window, wishing the floor would swallow me whole, feeling thoroughly put on the spot and deeply unsettled by the attention.
